entry.title: Premium Hub CoE – DSC Knowledge Bits – New Migration Guide LE-WM to EWM on S/4HANA
entry.content:
In this blog, I would like to share the latest updates on the new migration guide that was released for supporting customers and consultants during planning the migration from SAP ERP LE-WM or SAP S/4HANA Stock Room Management to EWM on S/4HANA. 

Additionally, I would like to clear up some confusion about end dates of usage rights and mainstream maintenance for SAP LE-WM.

LE-WM Roadmap - Important Dates:
Let's start with the SAP LE-WM roadmap and timeline, as this is mainly dependent on the ERP solution release on which LE-WM is running.

  • LE-WM on ERP ECC:
    SAP will provide mainstream maintenance until 31.12.2027 for SAP Business Suite 7 core applications, refer to SAP Note 2881788 for details.

  • LE-WM on ERP S/4HANA:
    SAP restricts the usage of components which are in a compatibility mode until 31.12.2025, refer to SAP Notes 2269324, 2881166, and 2577428 for details.

Migration Guide Overview:
In this migration guide you will find information about:

  • General Process Changes between LE-WM and EWM
  • System Setup Guides
  • Organizational Structure
  • Customizing Settings for Product Master
  • Define Handling Unit Management
  • Storage Bin Migration
  • Putaway and Stock Removal
  • Material Master Migration
  • Transactional Data Migration
  • Stock Migration
  • Physical Inventory Completeness Migration

Out of Scope:

  • In general, transactional data is not transferred from LE-WM to EWM. So no documents can be open when the migration starts. For more information, see Transactional Data Migration.
  • Existing RF transactions are not transferred automatically from LE-WM to EWM.
  • System users and the related authorization roles and profiles are not transferred automatically from LE-WM to EWM.
  • The existing migration tools do not migrate Customizing. Due to the broader functionality in EWM, the Customizing differs from LE-WM. For more information, see General Process Changes between LE-WM and EWM.
  • Customer-specific objects are not transferred

SAP Note link where you can download the mgiration guide: https://me.sap.com/notes/3523591.

Thanks to the EWM product management and CoE teams for the preparation of this useful document.

Learn more about SAP Premium Engagement and how a global team of experts can help you to get the most out of your SAP solutions.

If you already are a Premium Engagement customer and you are interested in knowing more about SAP S/4HANA Supply Chain Extended Warehouse Management solution, contact your Technical Quality Manager (TQM) to arrange for Architecture Point of View (APoV) service.

#CoE EMEA SCM1

entry.title: Test System Refresh from Production copy with GDPR compliance
entry.content:
In today’s fast-evolving digital landscape, businesses rely on robust and up-to-date test systems to develop, test, and deploy new features and applications seamlessly. Also training staff using a training system which has data like production system, accelerates digital transformation and user adoption of new feature and software products.

One of the most efficient ways to achieve this is by performing a Test System Refresh using production data. However, organizations need to ensure that this process adheres to stringent data protection laws like the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) in Europe, ICO in UK, CCPA, PIPEDA in Americas, DPDPA in India and many others similar across the globe. When done correctly, production copy for tests and training offers multiple business benefits, enhancing both operational efficiency and compliance. In this post we explore, why refreshing test and training systems from production copies is a game-changer and how regulatory compliance plays a critical role in the process.

What is a Test System Refresh?

A Test System Refresh is the process of copying data from a live production environment to a test or development system. This refresh ensures that the data used for testing is as realistic as possible, reflecting the most current operational conditions. The result? Your test environments are well-aligned with production, enabling better testing, bug-fixing, and development of new features.

Why Refresh Test Systems with Production Data?

Using production data for testing offers several advantages:

  1. Realistic Data Scenarios: When developers and testers work with data that closely resembles real-world production data, the accuracy of tests improves dramatically. Scenarios can be tested with actual workflows, ensuring that any bugs or issues identified in testing are likely to be encountered in the real system as well. This leads to fewer surprises when rolling out new updates or features.
  2. Comprehensive Test Coverage: Production data is often far more complex than synthetic or dummy data. It includes various edge cases that might not have been considered during test data generation. By refreshing test systems from production, you ensure that your testing is comprehensive, reducing the risk of missing critical bugs or issues.
  3. Accelerated Development Cycles: Having accurate and up-to-date test environments allows development teams to work faster and more efficiently. Issues are easier to identify, troubleshoot, and resolve when developers are working in a realistic setting. This leads to shorter development cycles, faster feature releases, and improved time-to-market.
  4. Cost Savings: The ability to test with production data reduces the number of errors that slip into production. Fixing issues in production is far more expensive than addressing them during testing. By investing in regular test system refreshes, businesses can significantly reduce operational costs over time.
  5. Reduced Training Costs: When onboarding new employees or training existing ones on new features, having access to test systems that closely mirror production is invaluable. Realistic data helps employees understand the systems better, improving training efficiency and reducing the time it takes for employees to become fully productive.

 

GDPR and other regulatory Compliance: The Crucial Piece of the Puzzle

While refreshing test systems with production data offers significant benefits, it’s critical to ensure that this process complies with GDPR. The regulation mandates strict rules on how personal data should be handled, especially when it is transferred or copied to non-production systems. Failing to adhere to GDPR can result in hefty fines and damage to a company’s reputation.

So, how can businesses enjoy the benefits of production data in testing while staying compliant? Key Steps for GDPR-Compliant Test System Refreshes

  1. Data Anonymization and Pseudonymization: The most effective way to achieve GDPR compliance is by anonymizing or pseudonymizing sensitive personal data before transferring it to test environments. Anonymization ensures that the data can no longer be traced back to an individual, while pseudonymization removes the ability to link data to specific people unless the key to the pseudonymization is revealed. This protects individual privacy while allowing test environments to maintain realistic datasets.
  2. Role-Based Access Controls: Only authorized personnel should have access to the data in test environments. Implementing strict access controls and logging ensures that sensitive data is protected and that any potential breaches can be traced and contained.
  3. Data Masking: Sensitive fields such as names, addresses, or financial data can be masked during the copy process. While the data retains its structure and characteristics, the specific details are obscured, ensuring that developers and testers work with data that resembles real information but doesn't compromise individual privacy.
  4. Encryption: Ensure that all data copied from production to test systems is encrypted both at rest and in transit. This minimizes the risk of data being intercepted or stolen during the refresh process.
  5. Audit and Monitor: Regular auditing and monitoring of test environments ensure ongoing compliance with GDPR. Track who accesses the data, what changes are made, and any potential breaches or non-compliance issues.

 Business Benefits of GDPR-Compliant Test System Refresh

  1. Accelerated Digital Transformation: Digital transformation adoption is accelerated by reduced test cycles, reduced development cycles, and reduced training time for new users onboarding new digital systems.
  2. High satisfaction by end users: Availability of production copy for training and testing systems, leads to higher adoption of new features. It helps the users to adopt software and leave using old tools. User satisfaction being higher helps overall delivery of projects by IT teams.
  3. Enhanced Security: By adhering to GDPR requirements, you significantly reduce the risk of data breaches, which could result in both financial and reputational damage.
  4. Faster Compliance Audits: Regularly performing GDPR-compliant test system refreshes helps streamline your organization’s compliance audits. Having a structured, secure process in place for handling test data demonstrates a proactive approach to data protection, making compliance checks smoother.
  5. Increased Customer Trust for organisation: When customers know that their data is handled with care and protected, they are more likely to trust your organisation. A GDPR-compliant data refresh strategy ensures customer trust is maintained.

 Conclusion

Refreshing test systems with production data can revolutionise how businesses approach testing, development, and training. The use of realistic data not only boosts productivity and reduces operational costs, but it also ensures higher quality in software releases. However, the benefits of a test system refresh are only fully realized when GDPR compliance is a core part of the process. By anonymizing, encrypting, and securing test data, businesses can unlock the potential of test system refreshes while protecting individual privacy and staying compliant with the law.

By striking this balance, companies can accelerate innovation, improve operational efficiency, and ensure data protection—ultimately driving long-term business success.

entry.title: Updates on Conhecimento de Transporte Eletrônico: Simplified CT-e
entry.content:
Versão em português 

Hi everyone,

The SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, inbound invoicing option for Brazil team has released updates on the Conhecimento de Transporte Eletrônico (CT-e). The update complies with SEFAZ Technical Note 2024.002 1.01 Version - Simplified CT-e. This new version is planned to be in production on October 7, 2024, according to the tax authority.

SAP Document and Reporting Compliance - CT-e Technical Note 2024.002 1.01 Version (Simplified CT-e)

The CT-e Basic and CT-e Flex processes now support the Simplified CT-e XML file. You can implement these changes in the inbound invoicing option for Brazil by following the instructions on the SAP Note 3519421 and the SAP Note 3519422.

Check out the latest updates and features in our What's New session on the SAP Help Portal:

  • SAP S/4HANA
  • SAP ERP

For a step-by-step guide on how to use the SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, inbound and outbound invoicing options for Brazil, refer to the following SAP Help Portal pages:

  • SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, inbound invoicing option for Brazil
  • SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, outbound invoicing option for Brazil

You can sign up for email alerts when new SAP Notes are released or updated in the components listed below; just follow the instructions in SAP Knowledge Base Article 2171560.

  • CA-GTF-CSC-EDO-BR (Inbound invoicing option for Brazil)
  • LOD-LH-NFE (Outbound invoicing option for Brazil)

Use the regulatory change manager tool to find out if a solution to a legal change is planned. Take a look at SAP Knowledge Base Article 3421995 and the regulatory change manager page to understand how to use this tool.

Have you found this blog post useful? If so, choose “like” (kudos), share it with your colleagues, and follow me for similar content. You are welcome to leave your questions and read similar blog posts on the SAP Document and Reporting Compliance SAP Community. Don’t forget to follow the SAP Electronic Invoicing for Brazil (SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica) tag and the SAP Document and Reporting Compliance community to keep up with the latest NF-e news.

Thank you, and until next time,
Thiago

entry.title: Atualizações no Conhecimento de Transporte Eletrônico: CT-e simplificado
entry.content:
English version here 

Olá pessoal,

A equipe SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, opção de emissão de fatura de entrada para o Brasil entregou atualizações no Conhecimento de Transporte Eletrônico (CT-e). A atualização está conforme a Nota Técnica 2024.002 versão 1.01 da SEFAZ (CT-e simplificado). Essa nova versão entrará em produção em 7 de outubro de 2024, conforme a autoridade fiscal.

SAP Document and Reporting Compliance - Nota Técnica 2024.002 versão 1.01 da SEFAZ (CT-e simplificado)

Os processos CT-e Básico e CT-e Flex suportam agora o arquivo XML de CT-e simplificado. Você pode implementar essas modificações na opção de emissão de fatura de entrada para o Brasil seguindo as instruções na SAP Note 3519421 e também na SAP Note 3519422.

Confira as últimas atualizações e recursos na sessão What's New no portal SAP Help:

  • SAP S/4HANA
  • SAP ERP

Para um passo a passo sobre como usar o SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, opção de emissão de fatura de entrada e de faturamento de saída para o Brasil, consulte as seguintes páginas do portal SAP Help:

  • SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, opção de emissão de fatura de entrada para o Brasil
  • SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, opção de faturamento de saída para o Brasil

Você pode receber alerta por email quando novas SAP Notes forem lançadas ou atualizadas nos componentes listados abaixo; basta seguir as instruções do SAP Knowledge Base Article 2171560.

  • CA-GTF-CSC-EDO-BR (opção de emissão de fatura de entrada para o Brasil)
  • LOD-LH-NFE (opção de faturamento de saída para o Brasil)

Utilize o gerenciador de modificações legais para ter uma noção do que está planejado para ser suportado. Confira o SAP Knowledge Base Article 3421995 e a página Regulatory Change Manager para entender como usar essa ferramenta.

Gostou desse post? Dê um “like” (kudos) e compartilhe o conteúdo com seus colegas. Sinta-se à vontade para deixar suas perguntas no SAP Community e ler posts semelhantes no blogging space do SAP Document and Reporting Compliance. Para acompanhar as últimas notícias da NF-e, siga a tag SAP Electronic Invoicing for Brazil (SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica) e a comunidade SAP Document and Reporting Compliance.

Obrigado e até a próxima,
Thiago

entry.title: Maximizing ROI from Your SAP EWM Investment: Expert Strategies for Continuous Optimization
entry.content:
Logistics Directors,  IT Program managers,  wonder if they are getting the most out of your SAP Extended Warehouse Management (EWM) investment. If you feel like your warehouse operations aren’t fully optimized, you’re not alone. IT and logistics leaders often find themselves wondering if they’re truly maximizing ROI from their SAP systems. As a veteran with over three decades of experience in SAP implementations, I've seen countless organizations struggle to maximize their Return on Investment (ROI) from this powerful tool. In this article, we'll explore common challenges and proven strategies to unlock the full potential of your SAP EWM investment.

The SAP EWM ROI Challenge

Many organizations face similar hurdles when it comes to optimizing their SAP EWM systems:

  1. Inefficient processes that fail to leverage EWM's advanced capabilities
  2. Low user adoption due to inadequate training and support
  3. Limited data visibility, hindering informed decision-making

These issues aren't just minor setbacks – they're ROI killers. They result in slower order fulfillment, wasted resources, and widespread frustration across teams.

Expert Strategies for SAP EWM Optimization

Drawing from my 30+ years of experience, here are four key strategies to boost your SAP EWM ROI:

1. Harness Advanced Features

Task & Resource Management, RF Framework, Queue Management,  Slotting, Dynamic routing with POSC ( Process Oriented Storage Control) and LOSC ( Layout Oriented Storage Control),  Clustered Putaway, Wave Management,  Fixed Bin replenishment, Full pallet picking, Warehouse Monitor aren't just buzzwords. When properly implemented, they're game-changers for efficiency.

2. Conduct Regular Process Reviews

Your warehouse operations are dynamic, and your processes should be too. Implement quarterly workflow assessments to identify and eliminate inefficiencies.  SAP is heavily investing in EWM to roll out  new functionality with every release.  It is important to keep track of what is new and  what is relevant for continuous innovation of your EWM system.  Check this information at  SAP S/4HANA What’s New Viewer. Link to the viewer on SAP website is - https://help.sap.com/whats-new/5fc51e30e2744f168642e26e0c1d9be1  Please see below screenshot to filter it to EWM specific innovations.

 

 This ongoing optimization ensures your EWM system evolves with your business needs.

3. Prioritize Continuous Training

A one-time training session during implementation isn't enough. Develop a continuous learning program to keep your team proficient with SAP EWM. This approach not only improves system adoption but also empowers employees to leverage advanced features effectively. Encourage your subject matter experts (SME) to identify areas where users are making more mistakes and  focus on building focused training sessions on  those topics. These could be  scheduled as lunch and learn sessions as well without too much of training overheads.

4. Integrate Predictive Analytics

Combine SAP EWM with S/4HANA to unlock the power of real-time insights and predictive modeling. Some good examples could be open purchase orders / inbound deliveries / outbound deliveries to identify workload on warehouse. This integration can transform your decision-making  and planning capabilities, leading to smarter resource allocation and improved operational efficiency.

Remember, in the fast-paced world of logistics and supply chain management, standing still is equivalent to moving backward. It's time to optimize your SAP EWM investment and drive real, measurable ROI.

Are you facing challenges with your SAP EWM implementation? Share your experiences in the comments below, and let's discuss how to overcome them!

Shivaji Patil

SAP Warehousing Platinum Advisor

Genius Business Solutions, Inc.

entry.title: Cyber Security Protection for S/4HANA Backups with Enterprise Blockchain and SAP BTP Kyma 🚀
entry.content:
Welcome to the latest blog in the series on SAP and Enterprise Blockchain.

Hopefully you've all had a great summer and are full of energy and ready to go for the sprint to the end of the year, so let's bring all of our energy and get great things done.

In my journey with Enterprise Blockchain, I was talking to a CSP Telco in Norway who came with this Enterprise Blockchain use case which was new for me and a good one. It is easy to implement and extremely effective, difficulty is easy and impact is high, what's not to like 🙂 They've implemented it, by the way, and it works.

So, what's the scoop ?

As I previously wrote here Cyber Security and Resilience are no long about High Availability and Disaster Recovery, our HyperScalers have all of the capacity we could ever need and can spin up capacity on demand.

The threat these days is less that Hackers wants to steal our Data, and more that Hackers wants to simply mess up our Data so that our Business cannot Operate, rather than stealing they just want to put a spanner in the works  so to speak.

 

Credit: https://funkyenglish.com/idiom-throw-spanner-works/

 

Just imagine if a Hacker messed up the Data of an Airline, planes wouldn't be able to fly, if a Hacker messed up the Data of a Pharmaceutical company, there would be no medicines, if a Hacker messed up the Data of a Groceries company, there would be nothing to eat.

 

What's the Risk ?

In the same way that Fire Fighters prepare for fires, having the latest equipment and training, and assuming unfortunately that there will one day be a fire...

As part of our BCP Business Continuity Planning we must assume that one day our Organisation will be the victim of a Hacker, and the Operational SAP Applications will be down and will need to be Restored from Backup.

And here's the question, how can we be sure that the Hacker hasn't messed with our Backup File(s) ?

You want to Restore from Backup, but there is a doubt that the Hacker has messed with the Backup File, how can you be sure, which Backup file you can trust as the good one ?

And this is the Risk, can you Trust the originality and integrity of your Backup file(s) ?

 

What's the Solution ?

How can we guarantee that we know whether or not the Backup File(s) have been messed with ?

This is easy, we think about our use case or our demand and we look in our Enterprise Technology Standards for the technology which is the most suited to guaranteeing that Data can be trusted. 

And we find our Enterprise Blockchain Technology Standard.

So how can we use our Enterprise Blockchain which is running on the SAP BTP Kyma to guarantee that we know if we can trust the integrity and originality of our Backup files ?

This is so easy it's like boiling an egg,

tl;dr; we run a Hash over the Backup file and store the Hash output in the Enterprise Blockchain where it is super safely protected thanks to the special characteristics of the Blockchain (Immutable, Hash Mechanism, Distributed, Consensus Mechanism), and perish the thought, in the event of a BCP scenario, where we need to Recover with a Backup, we run a new Hash over the Backup file and compare the new Hash to the old Hash (which is safely  stored in the Blockchain to protect the integrity and originality of the Hash) and we see if the two Hashes are the same, if they are the same we are good to go with the Restore from Backup, if they are different then we go to the next Backup File and do the same again until we find a Backup File that we can trust.

And the long answer for those of us who are interested in the finer details...

Voila.... bring in the Enterprise Blockchain Platform Wallet. The best Enterprise Blockchain Platform products include what is called the Enterprise Blockchain Platform Wallet, or to make it shorter, the Enterprise Blockchain Wallet.

The Enterprise Blockchain Wallet enables us to store large Data, like large Files safely and securely off the chain, or 'Off-Chain'. 

But if we store the large Data files Off-Chain in the Enterprise Blockchain Wallet, then how do we also have them some how on the Enterprise Blockchain Database ?

The way this works is elegant, in any decent Enterprise Blockchain Platform, the Enterprise Blockchain Wallet location is completely configurable, and could be anywhere from SAP HANA Cloud (Data Lake), or for example multiple hyperscaler object stores, such as Amazon S3, OSS (Alicloud Object Storage Service), SAP HANA Cloud, Data Lake, and Azure Blob Storage.

The configurable Enterprise Blockchain Wallet of the Enterprise Blockchain Platform looks like this:

 

Enterprise Blockchain running on SAP BTP Kyma with Enterprise Blockchain Wallet

 

Ok, so we've got the Backup File stored in the (configurable) Enterprise Blockchain Wallet, but what about securing the Backup File Data ? Obviously the Enterprise Blockchain Wallet storage location has built in security, for example the SAP HANA Cloud, the AWS S3 Buckets, but we need more than the out of the box security of these products, the reason we are using the Enterprise Blockchain Database is because of the amazing security strengths that it natively out of the box has, and so, what about the Enterprise Blockchain Wallet, doesn't the Enterprise Blockchain Platform have some cool super hard way of protecting the data in the Enterprise Blockchain Wallet ?

Well yes it does, this is the magic of Enterprise Blockchain Database 'Off-Chain' storage in the Enterprise Blockchain Wallet. This is so unique to Blockchain Technologies.

What happens is this, when store data in the Enterprise Blockchain Wallet, the Enterprise Blockchain Platform software runs a hash algorithm over the data that we have stored and the data, and the large file gets hashed:

 

 

The Backup File in the Enterprise Blockchain Wallet gets Hashed, and then, that Hash is stored in the Enterprise Blockchain Database.

This means we now have a unique Hash of that Backup File, and if anybody or anything makes even the tiniest teeniest change to that data or file, next time we run a Hash over that data or file the result will be different that the original Hash which is safely stored in the Enterprise Blockchain Database and this is how we will know that the data has been changed and we cannot trust the Data and therefore we cannot use it for Restoring our System.

On the other hand, if just before we plan to run the Restore from Backup with the Backup File from the Enterprise Blockchain Wallet, if we run a Hash over the Data and the Hash result is the same as we have in the Hash which is stored safely in Enterprise Blockchain Database, then we will know we can trust the Backup File and we can use it for our System Restore from Backup.

 

Enterprise Blockchain running on SAP BTP Kyma protecting SAP S/4HANA Backup Integrity

 

 

And this is why, for all of these reasons, 

Trustable S/4HANA Backups depend of storing a Hash of the Backup File in the  Enterprise Blockchain

 

Enterprise Blockchain is:

. The Digital Transformation of Information Security 

. The Next Generation Data Integrity, Originality, Confidentiality Protection

. Re-imagining Information Security

. Natively, out of the box, due to its special characteristics the strongest, hardest, most resilient Enterprise Database product 

 

Enterprise Blockchain is both:

. a Secure Store

. a Secure Communication Channel

 

The configurable Enterprise Blockchain Wallet enables you to store Big Data 'Off-Chain' and the hashes of the Big Data are stored safely and securely on the Enterprise Blockchain Database.

And this is Why I love SAP and Blockchain Databases and why you should too 🚀

The good news is, as we discussed in the previous blog, this is no longer hype, we can do all of this today, and now, within the SAP Partner Edge Open EcoSystem there are enabling technology Blockchain Products designed and built by SAP Experts specifically for the needs of SAP Customers to make doing Blockchain and SAP easy, and so you can do SAP and Blockchain, today it's real and there's nothing stopping you. Here's a how to guide which you can use to try this today with the SAP BTP Kyma Trial and Enterprise Blockchain.

So what are we waiting for ? Oh yeah, more use cases, ok, that will be the next blog. 

What do you think, are the words AI, Blockchain, Web3, Distributed Ledger Technology, starting to appear in your Company's visions and technology visions ? What use cases are you looking at ? Let's chat about it in the comments.

For now, over and out.

Andy Silvey.

Independent SAP Technical Architect and CEO of atkrypto

Author Bio:

Andy Silvey is a 25 years SAP Technology veteran [15 years SAP Basis and 10 years SAP Tech Arch including Tech, Integration, Security, Data from 3.1H to S/4HANA PCE on RISE and the BTP and everything in between, and former SCN Moderator and Mentor alumni].

Andy is also co-Founder of atkrypto inc, an startup whose ambition is to make Blockchain easy for Enterprise.

atkrypto's flagship product is the atkrypto Enterprise Blockchain Platform for SAP,  and atkrypto is a SAP Partner Edge Open EcoSystem Partner. 

The atkrypto Enterprise Blockchain Platform for SAP has been designed by SAP Independent Experts for the needs of SAP Customers and to be deployed on the SAP BTP Kyma Runtime Service and leverage native integration to SAP Products.

atkrypto Enterprise Blockchain Platform for SAP has a number of unique qualities, including being the only Blockchain software in the world which has a DataCenter version and a light mobile version which can run on Edge/IoT/Mobile devices and enables data to be written to the Blockchain at the Edge where that same Blockchain is running on a Server in the DataCenter, protecting the integrity and originality of data from the Edge to Insights. Taking Blockchain to the Data at the Edge instead of taking the Data to the Blockchain.

entry.title: Generative AI use cases for SAP ABAP
entry.content:
Organizations that use SAP are familiar with flexibility as well as challenges associated with custom code. According to SAP’s review, 65% of custom code has not been used in past 12 months by several organizations. So, with SAP’s direction for organizations to move to a Clean Core, this is the right time to evaluate your custom code and understand the impact and opportunity going forward.

With considering move to S/4HANA and Clean Core, SAP customers are looking at following areas as priorities:

  • SAP Custom code understanding: people who originally wrote the code may not be accessible anymore and robust documentation doesn’t exist, so understanding the custom is one of the first steps towards a clean core future
  • Custom code decommissioning: purging code that is either not used anymore or for functionality that comes standard with S4HANA
  • Code transformation: transform SAP ECC code to S4HANA and decide which tools and technologies to use for extensions
  • Code Optimization: optimize for performance, efficiency, or resources
  • Code Review for best practices: ensuring new code meets organizational coding best practices, does not have any sensitive information, and follows SAP ABAP Cloud guidelines such as access only SAP Public APIs

Without talking about any company or model, we wanted to focus on whether Generative AI models are positioned to help with any of the above. With the disclaimer that Gen AI can hallucinate, and general training doesn’t imply that it would understand SAP ABAP code, we think of this as an experiment to talk about direction rather thanks absolute solution. So, we don’t recommend relying solely on the results of Gen AI and prefer to always have a human in the loop.

Gartner also has an insightful article that talks about when not to use Generative AI. 

Definitions

This section defines some of the Generative AI related terminologies we’ll be using in this article

  • Foundation Models (FM): Machine learning models that are trained on large amount of general unlabeled data set and can perform wide variety of tasks such as new text and image generation and natural language conversation. Transformer model used in Generative AI is capable of learning context and sequence of words.
  • Prompt: A question or set of instructions that you provide to AI to generate usable answer from an FM
  • Hallucination: Whether an FM knows the right answer or not, it produces some answer; hallucination is when it generates inaccurate or inconsistent answer
  • Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG): It is a way of grounding the response of the model by providing a knowledge base as input along with the prompt

Prerequisites

To test the use cases highlighted in this article, you can use any IDE and connect it to an FM such as Visual Studio (VS) code with Open Source Continue addon.

Prompt’s impact on output

You may already be familiar that the prompts guide the behavior of the FM and leading providers of FM such as Anthropic and OpenAI have published guidance about it.

So, we know that prompts matter and can be used to refine and customize the output. This article uses this idea to explore persona-based ABAP code documentation.

Persona based code documentation

If you are an ABAP programmer, you want more detailed explanation of the code including each class or function vs program manager who wants to understand what the code does at a higher level.

Examples of code explanation based upon these 2 personas (code is supplied as part of prompt)

Program Manager Persona:  

Prompt: Following is a custom SAP ABAP code. The person who wrote this left the organization and we are trying to figure out what this code does.

Output:  Here's a summary of what this custom SAP ABAP code does:

1. It's a report named ZPREDACCT_MOVE_RECORDS that handles data movement for predictive accounting.
2. The program takes a date input (FOR_DATE) from the user.
3. It checks if predictive accounting is enabled by querying the ZPA_SWITCH table.
4. If enabled, it retrieves percentage data from ZPA_PERCENTAGE table for the current month and next 6 months.
5. Based on the input date, it determines the current and next month for processing.
6. For each month (current + 6 future months), it:
   a. Retrieves data from ZDEMO_DATA table for that month.
   b. Calculates how many records to move based on percentages from ZPA_PERCENTAGE.
   c. Checks if records have already been moved for that month in ZPREDACCT_DATA.
   d. Randomly selects the calculated number of records from ZDEMO_DATA.
   e. Moves these records to ZPREDACCT_DATA and updates their status in ZDEMO_DATA.
7. After processing all months, it updates both ZPREDACCT_DATA and ZDEMO_DATA tables.
8. The program includes a helper function GET_NEXT_PERIOD to calculate next month's dates.

In essence, this program moves a configured percentage of records from ZDEMO_DATA to ZPREDACCT_DATA for predictive accounting purposes, doing so for the current month and 6 months into the future. The percentages vary by month based on the ZPA_PERCENTAGE table configuration.

Tip: Based on above output, it wouldn’t be a stretch to suggest that it can also be used to evaluate whether the code is needed going forward or not, thus accelerating your clean core initiative.

ABAP Programmer Persona

Prompt: You are an SAP ABAP coding expert, and following is a custom SAP ABAP code. The person who wrote this left the organization and we are trying to understand what this code does. The result will be reviewed by a programmer, so provide detailed technical output.

Output: This ABAP code appears to be a report program named ZPREDACCT_MOVE_RECORDS. Its main purpose is to move records from a source table (ZDEMO_DATA) to a target table (ZPREDACCT_DATA) for predictive accounting purposes. Here's a detailed breakdown of what the code does:

1. Program Initialization:
   - Defines a selection screen with a parameter FOR_DATE (default is current date).
   - Declares various internal tables and structures.

2. Selection Screen Validation:
   - Checks if the entered date is not in the future.

3. Main Processing:
   - Checks if Predictive Accounting (PA) is enabled by querying the ZPA_SWITCH table.
   - If enabled, it proceeds with the following steps:

4. Date Calculations:
   - Determines the current period and processing date based on the input date.
   - Calculates future months (up to 6 months ahead).

5. Percentage Retrieval:
   - Fetches percentage values for each future month from the ZPA_PERCENTAGE table.

6. Record Processing (for each future month):
   - Retrieves records from ZDEMO_DATA for the specific month.
   - Calculates the number of records to be moved based on the percentage.
   - Checks for existing records in ZPREDACCT_DATA for the same period.
   - Randomly selects records from ZDEMO_DATA to be moved, up to the calculated number.
   - Moves selected records to ZPREDACCT_DATA, updating their status in ZDEMO_DATA.

7. Data Updates:
   - Updates ZPREDACCT_DATA with the new records.
   - Updates ZDEMO_DATA to mark processed records.

8. Helper Function:
   - FORM get_next_period: Calculates the next period and its start/end dates.

Key Points:
- The program uses several custom tables: ZDEMO_DATA, ZPREDACCT_DATA, ZPA_SWITCH, ZPA_PERCENTAGE.
- It implements a predictive accounting scenario, moving a percentage of records to a future period.
- The process is repeated for up to 6 future months.
- Random selection is used to choose which records to move.
- The code includes both interactive and batch processing logic.

Potential Improvements:
1. Error handling could be enhanced, especially for database operations.
2. The code could benefit from modularization into smaller, more manageable functions.
3. Some hardcoded values (like 6 for future months) could be made configurable.
4. Performance optimization might be needed for large datasets.

This code appears to be part of a custom predictive accounting solution, likely used for financial forecasting or planning purposes within an SAP system.

 

Code review against organization’s standards

You can use this technique as a quality check for code review by supplying both code and your organizational guidance. Depending on your choice of tool, you can either upload these documents or provide as a prompt.

Prompt: You are SAP ABAP Coding guide. File ZPREDACCT_MOVE_RECORDS.txt has the SAP ABAP Code and file Org-Standard-guidance.pdf has the organization guideline. Can you review the code against the guideline and provide the compliance report

Output: Based on the review of the ABAP code in ZPREDACCT_MOVE_RECORDS.txt and the organizational guideline in Org-Standard-guidance.pdf, here is the compliance report:
1. Naming Conventions:
   - The program name ZPREDACCT_MOVE_RECORDS follows the standard naming convention for customer programs (starting with Z).
   - Variable names like ls_sales_order, lt_sales_order, lt_dbupdate_so, etc. follow the standard naming conventions for local and internal tables.
2. Code Structure:
   - The code is well-structured with proper event blocks (START-OF-SELECTION, AT SELECTION-SCREEN, etc.).
   - The code is modularized with the use of FORM routines (e.g., get_next_period).
3. Comments:
   - The code has adequate comments explaining the purpose and functionality of the program, as well as individual sections and routines.
4. Data Declarations:
   - Data types are explicitly defined for variables (e.g., TYPE dats, TYPE zdemo_data, etc.).
   - Internal tables are declared with proper data types (TYPE TABLE OF abaplist, TYPE TABLE OF zdemo_data, etc.).
5. Error Handling:
   - The code includes error handling and logging mechanisms using MESSAGE statements.
   - Appropriate system fields (sy-subrc) are checked for errors.
6. Performance:
   - The code appears to be following best practices for performance, such as using internal tables, avoiding unnecessary loops, and utilizing SAP standard functions (e.g., CALL FUNCTION).
7. Coding Standards:
   - The code follows the SAP coding standards for indentation, blank lines, and code formatting.
   - The code uses SAP standard constructs and statements (e.g., DATA, TYPE, PARAMETERS, CALL FUNCTION, etc.).
8. Alignments with Functional Requirements:
   - The code appears to be aligned with the functional requirements outlined in the Org-Standard-guidance.pdf guideline.
   - The code includes functionality for calculation, posting, and reversal, as well as portfolio movements and other specific requirements mentioned in the guideline.
Overall, the ABAP code in ZPREDACCT_MOVE_RECORDS.txt generally complies with the organizational guideline provided in Org-Standard-guidance.pdf. The code follows SAP coding standards, includes proper error handling and logging, and aligns with the functional requirements outlined in the guideline. However, it's important to note that a comprehensive code review would require additional checks, such as testing, code coverage analysis, and adherence to specific organizational coding guidelines (if any), which are not available in the provided files.

Improving output and accuracy

The outputs shown above are examples of what an output may look like, changing based upon your persona’s prompt. There are several techniques to improve the output, as follows:

  • Prompting techniques: Improving prompts using techniques such as multi-shot prompting (providing example to the model) has shown to provide better output and reduce hallucinations. Experiment with different prompts to find out which one matches your use case the best.
  • Model Evaluation: Evaluating the output of different FMs allows you to not only select the best model for the use case, but also decide about tradeoffs among output, price, and latency. Anthropic provides an evaluation tool allowing you to test different prompts, which you can use for metrics such as accuracy, robustness, and safety. 
  • Model Customization: this is a technique that lets you customize general FMs with your own data for specific domain. Model customization is generally time and cost intensive and we recommend extensive testing using prompting and model evaluation before you think about this option

What’s next

There is a lot more that goes into solving a real-world problem than this article talks about, and we wanted to provide our point of view about where and how to get started and how to think about using Gen AI for SAP Code.   

Feel free to add your feedback and experience in the comments!

entry.title: Kickstart SAP TechEd 2024 with Autonomous Finance and Maximize Your Business Efficiency
entry.content:
SAP TechEd 2024 is here! As we kick off for this pivotal event in the SAP calendar, anticipation is building around the revolutionary talks and groundbreaking ideas set to be unveiled. For this year’s fully virtual TechEd, I’m excited to share the new SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) use case, Autonomous Finance.

 

What is Autonomous Finance?

So, what exactly is autonomous finance, and why is it becoming a game-changer in the world of oCFO? Gartner states that autonomous finance is a “finance function where processes and activities are partly governed and majority operated by self-learning software agents that optimize front-, middle- and back-office operations." Powered by technologies like AI, machine learning, and automation, it will affect all parts of the finance function under the office of the CFO. 

So why should businesses be excited about autonomous finance? Let's examine some of its compelling advantages.

  1. Autonomy in finance drastically reduces human intervention, which translates into significant cost reductions for organizations.
  2. It also speeds up operations and boosts productivity.
  3. AI-driven analytics can provide deep, actionable insights from financial data, empowering businesses to make smarter, strategic decisions.

Take a look at this infographic on the trusted path to autonomous finance. 

 

Customer Success Stories

Several SAP customers are already tapping into the potential of Autonomous Finance to propel their businesses forward:

  • Connect departments: Endress+Hauser significantly boosted overall efficiency and simplified operations by utilizing SAP Integration Suite’s tools to standardize their data sources. Thanks to the suite’s APIs and capabilities, Endress+Hauser's customers can now effortlessly place equipment orders directly from their ERP systems. This, in turn, automatically generates a production order within Endress Hauser’s ERP system and forwards it to the production facility. 
    • Aligned priority use case: Deliver Connected Experiences - Accelerate integration and increase productivity
  • Analyze: ARAG adopted SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud to create a unified and efficient analytics solution, enabling easy data accessibility. The combination of these technologies enabled ARAG to make all their data more accessible across different countries and teams, which paved the way for faster gaining of meaningful insights.
    • Aligned priority use case 1: Integrate hyperscaler data with SAP Datasphere - Deliver insights using live data from SAP and hyperscalers
    • Aligned priority use case 2: Accelerate Financial Planning and Analysis - Enhance SAP S/4HANA with planning and analysis
  • Automate processes: Orbico's IT team implemented SAP Build Process Automation to automate specific processes and tasks, thereby reducing project times and costs. The team launched over 40 process automation projects within a year, including a bot that automated invoice processing from PDFs into SAP S/4HANA, recognizing invoices from 20 vendors.
    • Aligned priority use case: Increase efficiency and accuracy while reducing costs - Automate processes to empower finance professionals
  • Embed intelligence: To Mercedes-AMG PETRONAS Formula One Team, efficiency is key to success. Formula One has cost caps that limit each team’s spending on cars per season with severe penalties for overshoots, making financial management one of their biggest challenges. Mercedes-AMG PETRONAS Team will leverage SAP S/4HANA Finance and SAP Business AI to optimize resource management and cost forecasting.
    • Aligned priority use case: Enhance customer support using the power of generative AI - Improve operations and decision-making with the power of generative AI

 

How to get started

SAP Discovery Center is an incredible asset to any business. If you’re in search of ways to start deriving value with SAP Business Technology Platform for your business, this is the place to start. It empowers users to easily uncover, find, and embark on SAP Business Technology Platform use cases.

Why SAP Discovery Center?

  1. SAP Discovery Center offers a catalogue of hundreds of detailed and comprehensive missions. These missions cover numerous aspects of business to inspire you on your SAP BTP journey. For example, when you’re ready to embark on the Autonomous Finance journey, these Discovery Center Missions will help you to take each step and make it best suited to your business needs.
  2. It provides guidance on how to use the wide range of services offered by SAP. This step-by-step guidance means you can use SAP’s powerful suite of tools to the best of your abilities and utilize the Free Tier and/or Free Trial.
  3. It encourages learning and exploration. With SAP Discovery Center, you not only understand how a host of innovative technologies could potentially benefit your business but also how to implement them efficiently for the best results with the provided reference architecture.
  4. The collaborative feature of SAP Discovery Center is very important. Knowledge and experiences are shared, thus enhancing productivity and enabling synergy. You can see which existing SAP customers have already implemented the use case of your interest and connect with SAP experts if you have any questions on the Mission.

Using SAP Discovery Center means you’re leveraging not just a tool but a collaborative platform that guides innovation, encourages learning, and boosts efficiency. Make sure you're tuned in for the XP180 | Get started with SAP BTP: Discover and accelerate with SAP Discovery Center session by Nis Boy Naeve and Ingo Deck from SAP Discovery Center team to learn more. 

 

What else can I do with SAP Business Technology Platform?

If you are interested in understanding further what SAP Business Technology Platform can do for your business, check out our comprehensive use case one-pager. The document is designed to provide a clear outline of how SAP BTP can be utilized to enhance your business operations, per your department. Grasping the use cases will allow your business to leverage SAP BTP effectively to drive business performance and inform your strategy, discover new potential, and maximize your technology investment with our SAP BTP use case one-pager. Don’t forget to also check out SAP Business Technology Platform use cases on the website.

You can read further on the SAP Business Technology Platform Priority Use Case One-Pager on this blog article.

 

Get ready for SAP TechEd 2024

I hope this got you excited about SAP TechEd 2024. Don't miss this opportunity to be part of a transformative event, and make sure to register for the keynote session with Muhammad Alam. Register now to secure your spot, and add the following recommended sessions to your agenda.

  • DT100 | Unlocking the intelligent enterprise: Cloud ERP innovations for the future
  • DA180 | Explore SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud end to end
  • IN103 | Modernize and elevate to the cloud with SAP Integration Suite
  • XP206 | Achieve enterprise automation with SAP BTP solutions and SAP Signavio
  • AI194 | SAP Business AI and Joule: What’s in it for innovators? 
  • XP180 | Get started with SAP BTP: Discover and accelerate with SAP Discovery Center

 

We look forward to connecting with you!

entry.title: Benefit From SAP Master Data Governance on SAP S/4HANA 2023 Feature Pack Stack 02
entry.content:
Latest Innovations in a Nutshell

Continuing the blog series about updates on MDG innovations in SAP S/4HANA, I'd like to provide you with details on the latest innovations in SAP Master Data Governance on SAP S/4HANA 2023 Feature Pack 02. Highlights include:

  • Generative AI for assisted changes/summary of changes in central governance (classic mode) and for business partner processing (in cloud-ready mode) using Joule AI copilot.
  • Improved data quality analytics for business partner and product master data.
  • Enrichment of business partner from CDQ reference data​ as an integrated part of consolidation, mass processing, etc.

These come in addition to various continuous improvements in central governance (classic mode).

Innovations in Detail

Let's take a closer look at the innovations.

Inclusion of Generative AI in MDG

Generative AI is used for summary of changes and assisted changes in central governance (classic mode), and for optional business partner processing (in cloud-ready mode) initiated by a Joule dialog. 

The AI-generated summary of changes provides you with an overview of changes in natural language (using a large language model) enabling you to: ​

  • verify the completeness and/or correctness of master data changes.​
  • get a brief overview of the changes comprised in an MDG change request.​
  • avoid pointless revisions based on incomplete changes.​
  • have a non-technical summary of changes (in contrast to change documents).​
  • facilitate approver’s final checks.

Generative AI-based summary of changes

​The AI-assisted change lets you change master data attributes with as little effort and as few steps as possible so that applying changes to a master data object is time efficient. You can make prompts in natural language to make field changes.

The AI-assisted change in central governance is applicable for business partner and material, and the AI-generated summary of changes in central governance is applicable for business partner, material, and custom objects which do not use editions.

In addition, SAP Master Data Governance on SAP S/4HANA integrates with the AI copilot Joule, enabling queries in natural language and navigation to SAP Master Data Governance on SAP S/4HANA cloud-ready mode for follow-up actions on business partner (incl. supplier and customer) data. (This innovation can be consumed as of FPS01).

Joule integration with follow-ups in MDG (cloud-ready mode)

These initial steps of including Generative AI in MDG processes are planned to be continued to cover more use cases in MDG classic mode and cloud-ready mode. 

"Data Quality Analytics 2.0" for business partner and product master data

  • SAP Analytics Cloud comes with an enhanced dashboard for chief data officers or master data specialists to get a real-time quality abstract on the prevailing quality and history trends of product and business partner master data, providing:
    • data quality overview in 3-level reporting structure by category, dimension, and rule
    • data quality analytics for seven business partner segments, e.g., Business Partner Core Data, Customer Sales Data, and Supplier Purchasing Data, each with drill-down using master data attributes or/and organization unit e.g., Country, Company Codes etc.
    • data quality analytics for seven product master data segments, e.g., Plant data, Sales Data, and Classification Data, each with drill-down using master data attributes or/and organization unit e.g., Product Group, Plant etc.
    • embedded authorization control via live data connection
    • integration in MDG system for data remediation

Data quality overview in 3-level reporting structure: by category, dimension, and ruleDeep-dive data quality analytics on business partner in SAP Analytics Cloud

For more details about "Data Quality Analytics 2.0", read the SAP Community blog post by Kefeng Wang.

  • Analytics CDS views for master data quality of business partner and product provide data source (CDS views) for master data quality analytics of business partner and product using organizational unit and attributes in SAP S/4HANA.

Enrichment of business partner records from reference data as an integrated part of consolidation, mass processing, etc.

In cloud-ready mode, the enrichment of business partner records from CDQ reference data is enabled as an integrated and automated step within:

  • Single Processing​
  • Mass Processing​
  • Consolidation​
  • Federation processes

Mass enrichment of business partners with reference data in SAP S/4HANA

For more information about the enrichment of business partner records from reference data with full process integration, read the SAP Community blog post by Martin Steinhauser.

Integration of SAP Business Workflow as local workflow engine (cloud-ready mode)

As an alternative to BTP Workflow Service (subscription required), the SAP Business Workflow can be assigned to process steps in the process template configuration. It can trigger any custom-defined action after a process step (e.g. email notification).

Integrate SAP Business Workflow as local workflow engine (cloud-ready mode)

Continued improvements for central governance in classic mode (material data)

Increased effectiveness through enhanced copy function in search and use of validation rules for classification in central governance for material: 

  • You can copy a selected material from the Search Materials Result List and choose one of three different copying options: Only Basic Data, All Data, or By Organizational Levels. When selecting Only Basic Data, basic data, long texts, and classification are copied. Choosing All Data will result in the complete data from the template material being copied. If you select By Organization Level, a dialog box will appear allowing you to choose which organizational data should be copied.​ 

Copy function in material search
Additionally, if a change request exists for the template material, the changed data from staging will be included in the copied material.

  • You can use validation rules for classes (table KSSK) and characteristics (table AUSP), defined using the rule management functionality of MDG, data quality management to check data during change request processing.

Validation rules for classes and characteristics

Continued improvements for central governance in classic mode (business partner incl. customer and supplier data)

SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS02 comes with improved Partner Functions content display and relationship categories in business partner processing:

  • The relationship type allows you to classify the relationship category into different subdivisions. Accordingly, you can maintain the relationship type on the user interface for Relationships, Contact Persons, Shareholder Relationships, and their related details user interface.
  • In addition, there are more search criteria when using the F4 Help on the Partner field in the partner functions lists ERP Customer Sales Areas: Partner Functions or ERP Vendor Purchasing Organization: Partner Functions. The F4 help also includes Business Partner, Customer, Supplier and Maximum No. of Hits as search criteria. The UIBBs for Partner Functions include the column Assigned BP. 

UIBB Partner Functions

Maintaining the Relation Type

For an overview of the innovations outlined above, see also the What's New in SAP Master Data Governance on SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS 2 presentation. As you can see from these innovations, both classic mode in MDG and cloud-ready mode are continuously enhanced. The driver for innovations in classic mode is primarily the SAP Continuous Influence program, whereas cloud-ready mode in MDG is the strategic development path going forward.

To get the full picture of planned innovations in SAP Master Data Governance across the entire MDG solution portfolio up to 2025, see the SAP Roadmap Explorer.

To stay up to date, simply follow the SAP Community Topic Page for SAP Master Data Governance and join the Q&A and discussion forum.

entry.title: SAP S/4 Hana - Zero Downtime Option
entry.content:
I am working on Release Upgrade from S/4 Hana 2021 to SAP S/4 Hana 2023, I’ve been diving into the Zero Downtime Option (ZDO) and wanted to make it easier for everyone to understand this game-changing approach for software updates.

Why ZDO?

System downtime during updates can be costly and disruptive. Imagine upgrading your system without halting operations—ZDO makes that possible! It allows updates for SAP S/4 HANA without technical downtime by keeping users working seamlessly while the update happens in the background.

How ZDO Works?

  1. Up-time Processing starts with your original system (Release 1). Users work as usual.
  2. Once ZDO is initiated, users transition smoothly to a bridge subsystem, which mimics the source system while the update is prepared in the background.
  3. Transactional Consistency is maintained throughout the process, ensuring your data stays intact.
  4. The target release (Release 2) is then activated, with minimal disruption. After a short restart (just 5-15 minutes), users are moved to the new release without them even realising it.
    Key Benefit
  • Minimal downtime: A single restart, no database reboot.
  • Low resource footprint: Only necessary tables are cloned.
  • No extra licenses or tools required.

ZDO not only reduces downtime but also helps keep business operations flowing seamlessly, ensuring system availability while upgrades happen in parallel

#sap #sapcommunity #SAPS/4HanaUpgrades #S/4HANA2021TO2023 #S4Hana @#S/4Hana

entry.title: Maximizing M&A Success: How SAP Solutions Drive Growth and Effectiveness
entry.content:
Maximizing M&A Success:  How SAP Solutions Drive Growth and Effectiveness

Authored by:  Christina Grace, SAP Global Finance Value Advisor & Marie Trojani, SAP Global HCM Value Advisor

Mergers and acquisitions (M&A) are complex transactions that can significantly alter the trajectory of a business. While they present opportunities for growth, diversification, and enhanced market presence, they also come with inherent challenges that can hinder success. One critical factor in navigating these challenges is the effective integration of systems, processes, people, and cultures. SAP offers a robust framework that can significantly enhance a company’s effectiveness for companies pursuing an M&A strategy. This blog explores how SAP facilitates successful M&A outcomes in the areas of Finance and HR and discusses potential inflection points in the journey to ensure success.

Understanding the M&A Landscape

Before delving into how SAP can assist during the M&A process, it's important to understand the typical M&A landscape. The process generally involves several phases:

  1. Planning and Strategy Development: Identifying potential targets and assessing strategic fit. This includes an assessment of current systems, people, and processes in both organizations. The goal should be to identify gaps and overlaps that could impact integration.  This phase also involves engaging key stakeholders from both organizations to ensure buy-in and prepare teams for the upcoming changes.
  2. Due Diligence: Analyzing financials, operations, and risks associated with the target company.  This analysis should provide insights into the target company’s strengths and weaknesses.  During this phase, data management tools can be used to perform scenario modeling of the two companies, set KPI goals, measure compliance, and ensure data harmonization.  A risk assessment should be performed to evaluate potential risks associated with the acquisition. Identifying risks early allows for proactive management strategies. That’s also at this stage that you can evaluate the cultural fit between the two companies.
  3. Integration and Strategy Management: Merging systems, processes, people, and cultures post-acquisition. Develop a comprehensive integration roadmap that outlines timelines, responsibilities, and key milestones. This roadmap should leverage GenAI integration capabilities to ensure a seamless transition. Prepare a training and support plan to educate employees about new systems and processes. Effective training will minimize disruptions and foster a sense of ownership among staff.  Real-time monitoring of the integration process is essential and allows for quick adjustments if any issues arise.  Establish feedback mechanisms to gather input from employees about the integration process. Utilize these insights to refine strategies and address concerns promptly. Particularly, HR concerns can appear quickly around job redundancies, salary gaps, talent retention for critical roles and employee morale.
  4. Post-Merger Evaluation: Assessing the success of the integration and overall M&A performance. After integration, evaluate the performance of the combined entity using advanced analytics tools. Assess whether M&A goals have been met and identify areas for further improvement, realizing greater synergies, and operating more efficiently.  Perform a cultural integration review by assessing employee engagement and satisfaction.  This allows companies to gauge the success of cultural integration, to gather insights, and to address any lingering issues.

Each phase presents unique challenges that SAP addresses with agility, transparency, and scalability.

The Role of SAP in M&A

SAP provides a comprehensive suite of integrated solutions designed to enhance operational efficiency, drive revenue growth, harmonize data, and improve real-time insights and overall business intelligence. Here’s how SAP Finance and HR solutions can specifically help companies pursuing an M&A strategy:

Seamless Integration of Systems

Challenge: One of the biggest hurdles in M&A is integrating disparate IT systems from merging organizations. Incompatible systems can lead to inefficiencies, data silos, and disruptions in business operations.

SAP Solution: SAP’s integrated platform enables organizations to unify disparate systems under one umbrella. With tools such as SAP S/4HANA, businesses can achieve real-time data integration and consistency, allowing for streamlined operations across the newly formed entity.  In the HR community, harmonizing different payroll, benefits, and performance management systems requires strategic planning and meticulous execution to avoid disruptions and maintain employee satisfaction.  This seamless integration reduces downtime and ensures continuity of service, which is critical during transitional phases.

Efficient Data Management

Challenge: During an M&A integration, organizations face an overwhelming amount of data that needs to be migrated, analyzed, and integrated. Poor data management can result in costly mistakes and compliance issues.

SAP Solution: SAP’s data management tools, including SAP Business Technology Platform, provide robust capabilities for data migration, cleansing, and integration. These solutions ensure that data from both companies are harmonized and accessible, allowing decision-makers to derive insights quickly and effectively. Real-time analytics further enable businesses to identify trends and make informed decisions swiftly.

Streamlined Financial Reporting

Challenge: Accurate financial reporting is essential during M&A, both for compliance and for assessing the financial health of the combined entity. However, multiple financial systems and reporting standards can complicate this process.

SAP Solution: SAP solutions offer integrated financial management tools that facilitate streamlined reporting and compliance with international financial standards. With SAP Analytics Cloud, businesses can create real-time financial dashboards, enabling stakeholders to monitor KPIs and other financial metrics seamlessly. This capability is crucial for transparent communication with investors, internal stakeholders, and regulatory bodies.

Enhanced Operational Efficiency

Challenge: Post-M&A, organizations often encounter operational redundancies and inefficiencies due to overlapping processes and systems.  Furthermore, redundancies and restructuring can lead to workforce reductions, which may result in the loss of valuable talent and institutional knowledge.

SAP Solution: SAP’s process automation capabilities can help identify and eliminate redundancies. For example, with SAP Ariba, companies can enhance procurement processes, while SAP SuccessFactors can streamline human resources management. HR must manage these transitions delicately, ensuring fair and compliant processes while maintaining operational continuity. These solutions foster operational efficiencies that lead to cost savings and improved productivity across the organization. 

Change Management Support

Challenge: M&A transactions often lead to significant challenges to company culture, processes, and personnel. Concerns about the unknown and resistance to change can derail integration efforts. 

SAP Solution:  Fostering employee engagement and loyalty can be key to overcoming these obstacles. Initiatives to support change management, such as training programs and team-building activities, are essential but must be carefully tailored to address the specific needs and concerns of the workforce.  SAP offers tools to support change management, including SAP Enable Now, which provides training and support resources for employees.  This tool allows organizations to create custom training materials and resources that can facilitate a smoother transition. By preparing employees for the changes ahead, companies can mitigate resistance and foster a more collaborative environment.

Risk Management and Compliance

Challenge: M&A transactions are inherently risky, and the complexities of integrating different compliance standards can lead to potential legal and regulatory issues.

SAP Solution: SAP’s risk management tools, such as SAP GRC (Governance, Risk, and Compliance), enable businesses to assess and manage risks effectively. These solutions provide a framework for identifying compliance gaps and ensuring adherence to regulatory requirements, thereby protecting the organization from potential liabilities.

Cultural Integration

Challenge: Merging different company cultures can be one of the most challenging aspects of M&A. Misalignment can lead to employee dissatisfaction and turnover.  M&A often herald significant organizational transformation, presenting numerous challenges for HR and employees. Merging distinct corporate cultures can lead to misalignment, confusion, and conflicts. HR must navigate the complex task of creating a cohesive environment where employees from both organizations feel valued and understood.

SAP Solution: While SAP solutions primarily focus on operational aspects, they can also support cultural integration indirectly. Tools like SAP SuccessFactors provide insights into employee engagement and performance, enabling leadership to address cultural challenges proactively. By fostering open communication and collaboration through these platforms, organizations can work towards aligning their cultures more effectively.

Job Security

Challenge: Another critical issue is job security. Employees often face anxiety and uncertainty about their future roles, leading to decreased morale and productivity. There is a higher risk for top talent to leave the company during times of uncertainty.

SAP Solution: Transparent communication from HR is vital to mitigate these concerns, as well as a comprehensive view into employee data such as competencies and job profiles. Analytics and AI can facilitate informed decisions and proactively alert if there are potential risks. Yet finding the balance between transparency and confidentiality can be challenging.

In navigating all of these challenges, Finance, HR and IT all play a crucial role in facilitating a smooth transition, fostering a positive organizational culture, and maintaining employee well-being and productivity during M&A activities.

Benefits of SAP Solutions in M&A

  • Efficiency: Streamlines operations and reduces integration time
  • Cost Savings: Minimizes redundancies and operational costs
  • Real-Time Insights: Provides data-driven decision-making capabilities
  • Risk Mitigation: Enhances compliance and governance
  • Employee Engagement: Supports smooth cultural integration through training and engagement tools

Conclusion

In today’s fast-paced business environment, the success of M&A hinges on effective integration and management of systems, people, data, and processes. In an era where adaptability and efficiency are paramount, embracing SAP Finance and HR solutions not only supports immediate M&A goals but also lays a solid foundation for sustained success in an ever-evolving marketplace.

SAP offers a comprehensive toolkit for companies navigating the multifaceted complexities and challenges of M&A, enabling organizations to streamline operations, enhance decision-making, and drive overall success.  By leveraging SAP’s integrated tools, companies can navigate the complexities of M&A with greater confidence and effectiveness. By facilitating integration, enhancing data management, and supporting operational efficiency, SAP helps organizations achieve their M&A objectives while laying the groundwork for long-term success.  As organizations continue to pursue growth through acquisitions, those that adopt SAP solutions will be well-positioned to realize the full potential of their M&A strategies, fostering a more agile and competitive future.

entry.title: SAP Commerce Cloud Q3 ‘24 Release Highlights
entry.content:
In Q3 2024, we've been hard at work to expand and enhance our unified commerce solution with new capabilities, including new AI functionality as well as core platform improvements. This will ensure that our customers operate in a secure, compliant and scalable manner, allowing them to focus their energy on their business. From zone redundancies to self-service premium disaster recovery, these backend improvements add an extra layer of stability and resilience, ensuring your commerce operations run without a hitch.

Our new AI functionalities are aimed at boosting efficiency. We've added AI Image Generation to the SAP CX AI Toolkit and announced the ChannelEngine partnership at SAP CX Live for AI-powered marketplace management. We'll delve deeper into this exciting partnership in this post.

The SAP CX Live virtual event also showcased how our comprehensive SAP Customer Experience portfolio helps your customers get more of what they want. For an in-depth look at the business advantages of using SAP CX solutions with SAP ERP, check out the new paper, Analyzing the Economic Benefits of Using AI-Powered SAP CX Solutions Natively Connected to SAP ERP.

Now, let's explore the standout features we've introduced over the past three months for SAP Commerce Cloud. Don't miss our Q3 2024 Release Highlights webcast for all the latest innovations.

 

Insightful

Merchandising your products just got easier with AI

Have you ever considered using AI to automatically create product images to show to customers? Wouldn’t that save you time and resources? Well, SAP Commerce Cloud merchandising capabilities just got even better with the addition of the Product Image Designer. The AI Image Generation tool lets you automatically create captivating new product images from simple text descriptions. Plus, the Background Editor allows you to transform or remove existing product image backgrounds, staying on-brand and boosting engagement with visuals tailored for commerce catalogs.

These enhancements are designed to make your life easier, letting you focus on what you do best while ensuring your product images look top-notch!

Product Image Designer

 

Adaptive

Boosting resiliency through enhanced cloud operations

SAP Commerce Cloud provides global hosting on Azure, where the platform can be hosted in your region of choice. While each region is divided into multiple availability zones, what happens if one availability zone has a disruption? This is where zone redundancies and Premium Disaster Recovery come in.

The perks of zone redundancies include bolstered resilience and security for your data centers, greater reliability for clusters, and increased availability for critical workloads such as search and storefront operations.

In Q2, we rolled out zone redundancies for Azure SQL databases. This feature automatically replicates databases across different zones within the same region, ensuring that a disruption won’t jeopardize your production environment.

For Q3, we’ve added zone redundancies for the SAP Commerce Cloud application along with key infrastructure, such as compute and load balancers. Keep an eye out for zone redundancies on storage which is coming soon!

We are thrilled to also announce that Premium Disaster Recovery is now available for self-service purchase in SAP Commerce Cloud through your Consumption Credits, a "virtual currency" we introduced earlier this year. You can log in to your cloud portal and assess this feature’s applicability in your enterprise through the updated estimator tool. From there, you can subsequently schedule and manage your upgrade from Standard to Premium Disaster Recovery.

Our systems are already designed with full backup capability in case of a major disruption. If a black swan event were to occur, Premium Disaster Recovery dramatically reduces both the Recovery Point Objective (RPO) and Recovery Time Objective (RTO). In a nutshell, RPO is the amount of data that would be lost, while RTO is the length of time it takes to recover and fully be back online. With Premium Disaster Recovery, the data loss and maximum amount of time to be back to regular online operations are both reduced by at least 75%.

These improvements aim to give you greater control and peace of mind, ensuring your business can bounce back swiftly from any disruptions.

Generate shareable links at a click of a button with Backoffice Deep Linking

Last year, we introduced Backoffice deep linking, and we're excited to take it up a notch with automated link generation. Now, you can easily generate URLs to bookmark and navigate directly to specific pages and items within Backoffice.

Share these links effortlessly with your team, agencies, and other colleagues to boost productivity and collaboration. This update will save you the hassle of manually navigating to specific pages every time. Enjoy a smoother, more efficient workflow!

Improve customer service and site security with Composable Storefront enhancements

Our Composable Storefront now comes with exciting new features designed to elevate customer service and enhance site security.

The Assisted Service Module has been updated to help your customer service agents shine. With just one click, agents can navigate from the customer list to a selected customer's 360-degree view. Switching back to the customer list to check other details is just as seamless. Additionally, the module now allows agents to search using the order ID and apply site filtering, making it a breeze to view order details and product pages for customers.

On the security front, the Composable Storefront now includes a verification step (think "I am not a robot") during customer registration to bolster site security. This comes with preconfigured support for Google reCAPTCHA v2. This added layer of security prevents brute-force attacks in registration and prevents bots from spamming websites.

These updates are all about making customer interactions smoother and more secure, making it a win-win for everyone!

 

Connected

Get a better handle over your order lifecycle with the latest SAP Order Management Services update

We're excited to introduce an option for synchronous integration between SAP Commerce Cloud and SAP Order Management Services that allow for faster and more efficient order processing, real time visibility and reduced overall operational costs.

To further assist you with managing large orders, we've also launched workflow loops. This feature simplifies orchestration by allowing you to apply sophisticated workflows to an even broader set of order data with significantly fewer steps.

Additionally, we've introduced the ability to include reasons for any Order Activity types, such as the rationale behind a delivery failure. This functionality is available for all standard and custom Order Activity types. Reason analysis is a crucial step towards optimizing the order lifecycle, and we're here to support you in achieving that goal.

Loop Control workflow for SAP Order Management Services

Harness AI to optimize marketplace management across 950+ sales channels

The SAP Store has expanded with new innovations and services, enriching our digital experience portfolio. We're thrilled to spotlight our partner, ChannelEngine, which empowers customers to connect and sell products on over 950 different online sales channels and international marketplaces. This solution is designed to maximize conversion rates, increase average order value, and boost customer engagement.

ChannelEngine provides brands, retailers, and distributors with the advanced technical capabilities needed to navigate the complexities of commerce management. By combining the strengths of SAP OMS and ChannelEngine, you can operate commerce unbounded across the globe, with centralized shopper order processing and access to rich insights into consumer demand, sales performance, and market trends. This partnership helps enhance unified commerce decision-making, making your business smarter and more agile.

ChannelEngine connects with 950+ online sales channels

 For more details on the above release features, check out the following resources:

  • Q3 2024 SAP Commerce Cloud Release Readiness Kit
  • Performance Optimization Best Practices
  • SAP Commerce Cloud update cycle
  • SAP Commerce Cloud Release Navigator single access point to release content

SAP Help Portal

  • SAP Commerce Cloud What’s New

Please share your feedback in a comment and follow our SAP Commerce Cloud topic page to connect with the community!

entry.title: 📢 Transition from TS410 to Cloud-Based IE2EE
entry.content:
SAP University Alliances member institutions have long offered SAP certification to highlight students' SAP expertise. The "Integrated Business Processes in SAP S/4HANA (TS410)" course will retire on June 30, 2025 and be replaced by "End-to-End Business Processes for the Intelligent Enterprise (IE2EE)" with SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, which is now available. SAP University Alliances will provide next steps for users of TS410. 

📢 For new certifications, start with the cloud-based course

entry.title: DTV New Release Announcement: DTV 1.5 released to customers
entry.content:
